sources_exist.note: All 7 evidence_facts[].url occurrences were opened live and resolved, including the two separately inventoried BLS occurrences.

figures_match_sources.note: All 7 atomic quotes appear exactly and contiguously on their live pages; titles, attribution, population, geography, unit, period, and disclosed industry mismatches are consistent with the source context.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The LOW_CONFIDENCE flag is appropriate and all 12 ESTIMATE_HEAVY packets were audited, but inputs.pi_dist uses inspection-contingency waivers, a demand-survival mechanism, to select an operator cost-savings-retention value despite having no payer, fee-setting, repricing, or pass-through evidence.

rubric_consistent.note: Template/finalizer identity, exact deterministic dataset objects, required SOC/title/wage-share rows, residual, provenance mapping, t50 boundary, role contributions, factor arithmetic, S, and both assigned Stage-4 flags all recompute and conform.

cross_checks_honest.note: F7's inspection-waiver mechanism is used in inputs.pi_dist and inputs.pi_moat and then reused in cross_checks.terminal_value and cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation, conflating capture with terminal paid-demand survival and violating the no-double-count requirement.

source_audits.note: Live BLS page contains the exact F1 home-inspector dwelling/condition quote. It is a U.S. occupational description broader than private NAICS 541350, and the quoted duty has no separately reported period.
The second inventoried occurrence was opened live; the BLS page contains the exact F2 worksite/office/reporting/scheduling quote and supports the disclosed broad occupational scope.
Live Palmtech page contains the exact F3 sentence about scheduling and AI-smart photo-review/documentation tools; it is correctly scoped as a vendor feature claim, not an adoption measure.
Live WIN FAQ contains the exact F4 statement of 279 franchise owners as of the end of 2024; the record correctly discloses that this is one franchisor rather than a market-wide acquirer count.
Live HouseMaster page contains the exact F5 1979 origin and first/oldest U.S./North American franchise-system statement; it does not establish a NAICS-wide consolidator count, as disclosed.
Live AmeriSpec page contains the exact F6 individually owned and operated franchise-owner sentence and identifies service availability across the USA; it is one network, not a consolidation census.

Live NAR article contains the exact F7 21%, 18%, and 17% inspection-contingency-waiver figures and identifies the September observation as September 2025; it measures buyer transaction behavior, not pricing or labor.

reasons: inputs.pi_dist: F7 measures whether buyers waive an inspection contingency, not how savings from AI-assisted report work are divided on inspections that are purchased. The record itself says payer, fee basis, and hours-based repricing are unverified, so the bridge to value 0.65 is not mechanistically supported. Rebuild this input from payer/fee/pass-through evidence or a candid independent capture estimate whose bridge excludes demand-survival effects.

inputs.pi_dist, inputs.pi_moat, cross_checks.terminal_value, and cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: the same F7 waiver mechanism is used to reduce capture/moat and again to classify terminal demand as PRESSURED. Separate transaction-level savings capture from mature paid-demand survival, allocate the waiver mechanism without double counting, and then re-evaluate the terminal narrative with an explicit mature-AI demand mechanism.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 12 inputs with provenance_type ESTIMATE (active_consolidators, ai_replaceable_share, buy_mult, current_adoption_pct, exit_mult, historical_analogs, owners_60plus_pct, pi_dist, pi_moat, pricing_structure, t50_years, owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented)
